title: memory parameter for CreateContainer varlink API does not have any effect
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  sudo varlink call unix:/run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.CreateContainer '{"create": { "name": "test", "image": "busybox","command": ["sh"], "resources": {"memory":1048576} }}'

2. sudo podman inspect --format '{{.HostConfig.Memory}}' test

**Describe the results you received:**
Verify that memory is not configured as expected but instead it's 0, which leads to no memory limit.


**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected to have Memory: 1048576 as I configured.
If I run the container with podman run I can limit the memory as expected.
```sudo podman run -m 1G busybox sh```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.11.4
Git Commit:    "49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"
Built:         Mon Jan 14 21:38:17 2019
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev

      commit: d164d9b08bf7fc96a931403507dd16bced11b865
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 6039793664
  SwapTotal: 6039793664

  cpus: 4
  hostname: sequoia
  kernel: 4.20.5-200.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
```

comments.body: Could you see if you could compile the patched version and if fixes your issue.
@rhatdan I confirm it works with the patch. Thanks!
